steps.title: Pin down what the conclusion requires
steps.content:
The conclusion is that EET is more effective than EHP but the result took time to become apparent. Any data that strengthens this must show (1) EET and EHP performing similarly early on, and (2) EET clearly outperforming EHP later.
  • Choice A — Productivity loss for the EET group barely decreased between 12 weeks and 12 months after the program began, while productivity loss for the EHP group significantly increased during the same time period.: This says EET barely changed while EHP got much worse — EET’s advantage would be visible early, not delayed. This contradicts “took time to become apparent.” ✗
  • Choice B — Productivity loss was largely due to absenteeism for the EHP group at 12 months after the program began, while productivity loss was largely due to presenteeism for the EET group at 12 months after the program began.: This describes the type of productivity loss (absenteeism vs. presenteeism) rather than whether the overall difference emerged gradually. It does not address the timing of EET’s advantage. ✗
  • Choice C — Productivity loss was consistently higher for the EHP group than for the EET group over the twelve months that it was measured, though the size of the difference between the two decreased over that time.: This says EHP was always worse and the gap was shrinking — neither matches a delayed advantage for EET. ✗
  • Choice D — Productivity loss was fairly similar for the EET and EHP groups 12 weeks after each program began, but at 12 months afterward it had significantly increased for the EHP group and significantly decreased for the EET group.: Groups start nearly equal at 12 weeks, then diverge sharply by 12 months. This is exactly what “took time to become apparent” predicts. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the pattern
steps.content:
The table shows EET at 268 and EHP at 282 at 12 weeks (nearly identical), then EET drops to 171 while EHP rises to 436 at 12 months. That sharp divergence after an initial tie is the delayed-advantage pattern the conclusion describes.

D is correct.
